title: Visual Basic Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Visual Basic commands and syntax for software engineers.

commands.description: Declare an integer variable named x.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to declare an integer variable.

commands.command:
If x > 10 Then
    Console.WriteLine("x is greater than 10")
End If
commands.description: Conditional statement to check if x is greater than 10.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to perform actions based on a condition.

commands.command:
For i As Integer = 1 To 10
    Console.WriteLine(i)
Next
commands.description: Loop from 1 to 10 and print each number.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to iterate over a range of numbers.

commands.command:
Sub Main()
    ' Your code here
End Sub
commands.description: Define the entry point of a VB program.
commands.scenario: Use to define the main procedure in a VB application.

commands.command:
Function Add(a As Integer, b As Integer) As Integer
    Return a + b
End Function
commands.description: Define a function to add two integers.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to create a reusable function for addition.

commands.command:
Try
    ' Code that may cause an exception
Catch ex As Exception
    Console.WriteLine(ex.Message)
End Try
commands.scenario: Use when you need to handle runtime errors gracefully.

commands.description: Declare and initialize an array of strings.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to store multiple string values in an array.

commands.description: Loop while a condition is true.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to repeat actions while a condition holds true.

commands.command:
Select Case day
    Case "Monday"
        Console.WriteLine("Start of work week")
    Case "Friday"
        Console.WriteLine("End of work week")
    Case Else
        Console.WriteLine("Midweek")
End Select
commands.description: Select Case statement for multi-way branching.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to choose between multiple options.

commands.command:
Class Car
    Public Property Make As String
    Public Property Model As String
End Class
commands.description: Define a simple class with properties.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to create a class to represent an object.

commands.command:
Module Module1
    Sub Main()
        Console.WriteLine("Hello, World!")
    End Sub
End Module
commands.description: Basic structure of a VB program with a module and main subroutine.
commands.scenario: Use as a starting point for any VB console application.

commands.command:
Public Sub New(make As String, model As String)
    Me.Make = make
    Me.Model = model
End Sub
commands.description: Constructor for a class to initialize properties.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to create a constructor for initializing class properties.
